steps.title: Identify the central contrast
steps.content: Defoe contrasts how far each form of communication reaches: the sermon “extends no farther than the strength of memory can convey it,” while the book “conveys its contents for ages to come, to the eternity of mortal time.” This contrast in permanence is the passage's decisive signal.

steps.title: Find the choice that captures the contrast
steps.content:
“Words committed to print have a greater permanence than messages that are merely spoken aloud.” This captures Defoe's contrast between sermons and printed books. The other three choices either reverse this relationship, introduce unrelated claims, or misread the passage.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
